audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with hallelujah, wedding, rider imagery; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Babylon aftermath, Lamb wedding, testimony of Jesus, rider Faithful/True, word-sword, beast defeat.

verses.restored: After these things I heard as great voice of many crowd in heaven saying: Hallelujah. Salvation/healing and glory and power of our Divine,
because true and righteous/just are his judgments, for he judged the great prostitute who corrupted earth in her misuse, and avenged blood/life of his servants from her hand.
And a second time they said: Hallelujah. Her smoke goes up into ages of ages.
The twenty-four elders and four living creatures fell and worshiped the Divine sitting on throne, saying: Amen. Hallelujah.
A voice came from throne saying: Praise our Divine, all his servants, those revering him, small and great.
And I heard as voice of many crowd, like sound of many waters and strong thunders, saying: Hallelujah, because Lord Divine Almighty reigns.
Let us rejoice and exult and give glory to him, because wedding of Lamb came, and his bride prepared herself.
It was given to her to clothe herself in bright clean fine linen; for fine linen is righteous/just acts of holy ones.
He says to me: Write: Blessed are those called to supper of wedding of Lamb. And he says: These are true words of Divine.

I fell before his feet to worship him, and he says: Do not do that. I am fellow servant with you and your siblings holding witness of Jesus. Worship the Divine. For witness of Jesus is spirit of prophecy.
And I saw heaven opened, and look, white horse, and the One sitting on it called Trustworthy and True, and in righteousness/justice he judges and wars.
His eyes are flame of fire, and on his head many crowns, having name written that no one knows except himself.
He is clothed in garment dipped in blood/life, and his name is called Word of Divine.
The armies in heaven followed him on white horses, clothed in white clean fine linen.
From his mouth goes sharp sword, so that with it he may strike nations, and he will shepherd them with iron rod; he treads winepress of wine of wrath of anger of Divine Almighty.
He has on garment and on thigh a name written: King of kings and Lord of lords.
I saw one messenger standing in sun, and he cried with great voice to all birds flying in mid-heaven: Come, gather to great supper of Divine,
to eat flesh of kings, commanders, strong ones, horses and riders, and flesh of all, free and enslaved, small and great.
And I saw beast and kings of earth and their armies gathered to make war with the One sitting on horse and with his army.

The beast was seized, and with it the false prophet who did signs before it, by which he misled those receiving mark of beast and worshiping its image; the two were cast alive into lake of fire burning with sulfur.
The rest were killed by sword from mouth of the One sitting on horse, the sword going from his mouth, and all birds were filled from their flesh.

translation_notes: The rider's sword comes from mouth: word/judgment imagery governs the scene.
Wedding supper contrasts with carrion supper: communion with Lamb versus collapse of beastly power.
Do not make this a literal battle fantasy; apocalyptic image shows defeat of beast/false-prophet systems.
safety_note: Revelation 19 contains violent symbolic imagery. Interpret through the Lamb's witness and word-sword; do not use it to fantasize harm against people.
